panels.title: QWEN tokens over selected range (in / out)

targets.expr: avg by (name) (avg_over_time({container=~".*assess-bot.*"} | json | evt=`phase` | ms!=`` | unwrap ms [$__interval]))

panels.title: Auth events over time (ok / fail / locked)
Cost — per assessment / per day / all-time
Cost — today (24h)
panels.description: AI (DeepSeek/QWEN) spend on assessments completed in the last 24h.

panels.title: Cost — selected range (all-time)
panels.description: Spend over the dashboard's time range. Set the range to 'Last 1 year' for all-time.

panels.title: Avg cost / assessment
panels.description: Selected range: total AI spend divided by completed assessments.

targets.expr: sum(sum_over_time({container=~".*assess-bot.*"} | json | evt=`assess_done` | unwrap qwen_cost_usd [$__range])) / sum(count_over_time({container=~".*assess-bot.*"} | json | evt=`assess_done` [$__range]))

panels.title: Assessments (range)
panels.description: Completed assessments (assess_done) in the selected range.

panels.title: Cost per day (USD)
panels.description: One bar per day. Uses a 1d interval regardless of zoom.

panels.title: Cost per assessment — by company (selected range)
panels.description: AI spend attributed to each company assessed. Multiple runs of the same company are summed.

targets.expr: sum by (company) (sum_over_time({container=~".*assess-bot.*"} | json | evt=`assess_done` | unwrap qwen_cost_usd [$__range]))

panels.title: Cost — LIFETIME (all time, ledger)
panels.description: TRUE all-time spend, read from the persistent SQLite ledger on the colt_events volume. Each assessment appends a cumulative `cost_snapshot`, so this is correct even after Loki has aged out the original lines. Source of truth: `python cost_report.py`.

panels.title: Assessments — LIFETIME (ledger)
panels.description: Total assessments ever run, from the persistent ledger.

panels.title: Avg cost / assessment — LIFETIME
panels.description: Lifetime spend / lifetime assessment count, from the ledger.

panels.title: Tokens out — LIFETIME
panels.description: Cumulative QWEN/DeepSeek output tokens, from the ledger.

panels.title: Failed assessments (24h)
panels.description: Engine crashed before finishing. THIS is why 'requested' used to exceed 'completed' with no explanation — an unhandled exception never emitted assess_done. Click the table below for the reason.

panels.title: Failed (selected range)
panels.description: Crashes over the dashboard range.

panels.title: Hallucinated CVEs stripped (range)
panels.description: Times the model cited a CVE id that is NOT in the scan evidence, so the guard removed it before it reached a customer slide (e.g. deepseek writing CVE-2021-44244 for Log4Shell).

panels.title: LLM fallbacks (range)
panels.description: Every model in the chain failed -> the deck fell back to English templates.

panels.title: Why assessments failed (assess_error)
panels.description: company + exception + message + the source line. The engine now emits this on ANY unhandled crash, so a dead run is as visible as a successful one.

panels.title: LLM chain — who stalled, why, and what saved the deck
Model failovers (range)
panels.description: A model in the chain stalled/refused and we switched to the next one. The deck still came out full quality — this is the safety net WORKING. Table below says which model and why.

panels.title: Degraded decks — English templates (range)
panels.description: EVERY model in the chain failed -> no AI prose -> the deck fell back to templated English. This is the failure you actually feel (a German deck coming out half-English). Should be 0.

panels.title: Deck-quality rate (range)
panels.description: % of assessments where the AI actually wrote the prose. <100% means decks shipped templated.

panels.title: Runs that crashed (range)
panels.description: Engine died on an exception — no deck at all. See the Failures table.

panels.title: Every model attempt — model · why it failed · timeout · what we switched to
panels.description: The 5 Ws of an LLM stall: WHICH model, WHAT happened (timeout/429/403/bad-json), WHEN (ts), HOW LONG we waited (timeout_s), and WHERE it went next (next_model). This is emitted per attempt, so a chain of 3 failures produces 3 rows.

panels.title: Raw engine log for failed / degraded runs (the actual lines)
panels.description: Unfiltered stream for any run that crashed or fell back — warnings, tracebacks, model errors. This is what you would otherwise SSH for.

panels.title: LIVE PROGRESS — every phase of every run (web + telegram)
panels.description: The engine writes each PROGRESS line to events.log as evt=progress with a pct. Previously it only print()ed them: promtail tails /logs/events.log and does NOT read stdout, so once colt-web started running the engine as a background task (its stdout is a pipe) live runs disappeared from Grafana entirely.

panels.title: Assessment phases over time (pct reached)
panels.description: Each run climbs 4 -> 8 -> 56 -> 62 -> 91 -> 99. A line that flatlines at 62% is enrichment hanging; flat at 8% is Shodan recon on a big estate.

targets.expr: max by (company) (max_over_time({container=~".*assess-bot.*"} | json | evt=`progress` | pct > 0 | unwrap pct [1m]))
